title: Event model
purpose: Define the Event enum — the spine of the event-sourcing system. Every recorded fact in a workflow's life is an Event variant. Events are type-erased: they carry Payload values (from AC-001) and error types (from AC-004), never a generic type parameter.
task: Define the event envelope (per-workflow sequence number, recorded timestamp, owning WorkflowId) and the Event enum with all lifecycle, activity, timer, signal, and child-workflow variants. User data is carried as Payload (AC-001); failure variants carry ActivityError / WorkflowError (AC-004). The enum SHALL NOT take a generic type parameter.

requirements.title: Event envelope
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an event envelope carrying a monotonic per-workflow sequence number, a recorded UTC timestamp, and the owning WorkflowId. Every event SHALL carry this envelope.
acceptance: event.rs defines an envelope (a struct field group or wrapper) with seq: u64, recorded_at: DateTime<Utc>, and workflow_id: WorkflowId.
The envelope is part of every Event value, accessible via accessors regardless of variant.
The recorded_at timestamp is the documented determinism source for workflow.now (a doc comment states this).
A unit test constructs an event and reads back its seq, recorded_at, and workflow_id.

requirements.title: Event variants
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define the Event enum with workflow-lifecycle, activity, timer, signal, and child-workflow variants. Variants carrying user data SHALL carry it as Payload, failure variants SHALL carry the AC-004 error types, and the enum SHALL NOT take a generic type parameter.
acceptance: Workflow lifecycle: WorkflowStarted (workflow_type + input Payload), WorkflowCompleted (result Payload), WorkflowFailed (WorkflowError), WorkflowCancelled (reason), WorkflowTimedOut (a timeout descriptor — a message or timeout-kind identifying which timeout elapsed).

Activity: ActivityScheduled (ActivityId + activity_type + input Payload), ActivityStarted (ActivityId), ActivityCompleted (ActivityId + result Payload), ActivityFailed (ActivityId + ActivityError + attempt: u32), ActivityCancelled (ActivityId).

ActivityFailed's attempt field plus the ActivityError classification (Retryable vs Terminal, from AC-004) are the discriminant the replay cursor uses to distinguish a retryable interim failure (followed by a later ActivityStarted/ActivityCompleted/ActivityFailed for the same ActivityId) from a terminal one — a doc comment states this so AD replay has the data it needs.

Child workflow: ChildWorkflowStarted (child WorkflowId + workflow_type + input), ChildWorkflowCompleted (child WorkflowId + result), ChildWorkflowFailed (child WorkflowId + WorkflowError), ChildWorkflowCancelled (child WorkflowId).

Cancellation is modelled as distinct events (ActivityCancelled, ChildWorkflowCancelled), symmetric with WorkflowCancelled and TimerCancelled, so the engine (AT-009) records a concrete cancellation event rather than overloading the failure variants.
Event has no generic type parameter; all user data is Payload, all failure data is ActivityError/WorkflowError.
The terminal lifecycle variants (WorkflowCompleted, WorkflowFailed, WorkflowCancelled, WorkflowTimedOut) are exactly the events the AC-003 status projection maps to terminal statuses.

requirements.title: Event serialisation round-trip
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL derive Serialize and Deserialize on Event and all its variants. Serialising an Event and deserialising it back SHALL produce an equal value.
acceptance: Event derives Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug, PartialEq.
A unit test round-trips at least one event of every variant through serde_json and asserts equality.
Enum representation is explicit (tagged) so the wire form is stable and forward-readable.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define Payload — it is the value carrier defined in AC-001 (a hard dependency of this brief).
SHALL NOT define ActivityError or WorkflowError — they are defined in AC-004 (a hard dependency of this brief, so the real error types are always available; there are no placeholders).
SHALL NOT compute WorkflowStatus from events — that is AC-003.
SHALL NOT define any storage trait.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
